title: Getting started with explodemap
text: explodemap generates hierarchical exploded-view maps dense administrative boundary data. applies rigid-body translations polygon geometries features visually separated feature’s internal geometry preserved exactly. basic two-level workflow : Group units regions using column data. Displace units within across regions using centroid-driven vector field analytically derived parameters. two-level core described paper, package designed satisfy three key properties: Proposition 1: polygon’s shape, area, perimeter, topology preserved exactly. Proposition 2: radial ordering units within region preserved. Proposition 3: unit displaced alpha_r + alpha_l metres.

title: Getting started with explodemap
text: explodemap expects sf object : Polygon multipolygon geometries grouping column identifying parent region unit projected CRS linear metre units Geographic coordinates EPSG:4326 (longitude/latitude) must transformed use. U.S. work, state plane, UTM, Albers-type projected CRS usually appropriate.
code: library(sf) #> Warning: package 'sf' was built under R version 4.5.2 #> Linking to GEOS 3.13.1, GDAL 3.11.4, PROJ 9.7.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE library(explodemap)

title: Getting started with explodemap
text: create small synthetic dataset four square polygons two regions.
code: sq <- function(xmin, ymin, size = 1000) {   st_polygon(list(matrix(     c(xmin, ymin,       xmin + size, ymin,       xmin + size, ymin + size,       xmin, ymin + size,       xmin, ymin),     ncol = 2,     byrow = TRUE   ))) }  geom <- st_sfc(   sq(0, 0), sq(3000, 0),       # Region A   sq(12000, 0), sq(15000, 0),  # Region B   crs = 3857 )  x <- st_sf(   id     = c("a1", "a2", "b1", "b2"),   region = c("A", "A", "B", "B"),   geometry = geom )  x #> Simple feature collection with 4 features and 2 fields #> Geometry type: POLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 0 ymin: 0 xmax: 16000 ymax: 1000 #> Projected CRS: WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator #>   id region                       geometry #> 1 a1      A POLYGON ((0 0, 1000 0, 1000... #> 2 a2      A POLYGON ((3000 0, 4000 0, 4... #> 3 b1      B POLYGON ((12000 0, 13000 0,... #> 4 b2      B POLYGON ((15000 0, 16000 0,...

title: Getting started with explodemap

title: Getting started with explodemap
text: default, explodemap derives displacement parameters analytically dataset geometry using paper’s two closed-form results. can also supply parameters directly. Overrides may supplied independently, can tune regional separation without changing local expansion, vice versa:
code: manual <- explode_sf(   x,   region_col = "region",   alpha_r = 100,   alpha_l = 200,   plot = FALSE ) #> Using manual alpha_r = 100 m #> Using manual alpha_l = 200 m  manual$params #> $alpha_r #> [1] 100 #>  #> $alpha_l #> [1] 200 #>  #> $p #> [1] 1.25 #>  #> $gamma_r #> [1] NA #>  #> $gamma_l #> [1] NA #>  #> $refine #> [1] FALSE more_region_gap <- explode_sf(   x,   region_col = "region",   alpha_r = result$params$alpha_r * 1.5,   plot = FALSE ) #> Using manual alpha_r = 2538.8531259649 m  more_region_gap$params #> $alpha_r #> [1] 2538.853 #>  #> $alpha_l #> [1] 2409.82 #>  #> $p #> [1] 1.25 #>  #> $gamma_r #> [1] NA #>  #> $gamma_l #> [1] 1.136 #>  #> $refine #> [1] FALSE

title: Getting started with explodemap
text: two-level algorithm clean paper model. dense municipal cores, can add bounded refinement pass nudges close -region neighbors apart analytical displacement: refine_max_shift caps extra correction per feature, refinement remains small display adjustment rather replacement displacement model. Use refine_within = "" remaining crowding crosses region boundaries.

title: Grouped layouts and anchor placement
text: explode_grouped() extends core two-level exploded-map workflow three-level hierarchy multi-region national-scale layouts. grouped extension useful standard two-level explosion still leaves region blocks visually crowded difficult compare across larger spatial extent. three levels : Level 1 — Local explosion. Units within parent region displaced using centroid-driven field core algorithm. geometric guarantees Propositions 1–3 apply level. Level 2 — Radial anchor placement. Parent region centroids displaced radially outward national centroid generate initial anchor positions region block. Level 3 — Collision-aware refinement (optional). Overlapping anchors iteratively repelled spring term maintains proximity original radial targets. Levels 2 3 preserve structural grouping directional correspondence rather topological coverage. formal geometric guarantees Propositions 1–3 apply strictly Level 1.
code: library(sf) #> Warning: package 'sf' was built under R version 4.5.2 #> Linking to GEOS 3.13.1, GDAL 3.11.4, PROJ 9.7.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE library(explodemap)

title: Grouped layouts and anchor placement
text: create small dataset six units three regions, spread across wider spatial extent two-region example vignette("getting-started").
code: sq <- function(xmin, ymin, size = 1000) {   st_polygon(list(matrix(     c(xmin, ymin,       xmin + size, ymin,       xmin + size, ymin + size,       xmin, ymin + size,       xmin, ymin),     ncol = 2,     byrow = TRUE   ))) }  geom <- st_sfc(   sq(0, 0), sq(3000, 0),       # R1   sq(9000, 0), sq(12000, 0),   # R2   sq(24000, 0), sq(27000, 0),  # R3   crs = 3857 )  x <- st_sf(   id     = paste0("u", 1:6),   region = c("R1", "R1", "R2", "R2", "R3", "R3"),   geometry = geom )

title: Grouped layouts and anchor placement
text: explode_grouped() supports three anchor placement modes:

title: Grouped layouts and anchor placement
text: simplest call uses mode = "auto": result grouped_exploded_map S3 object, also inherits exploded_map: Plot grouped layout:
code: g_auto <- explode_grouped(   x,   region_col = "region",   mode       = "auto",   plot       = FALSE ) #> Level 1: Applying local explosion (alpha_l = 2410 m)... #> Level 2: Computing anchor positions (mode = auto)... #> Applying anchor displacement...  class(g_auto) #> [1] "grouped_exploded_map" "exploded_map"         "list" print(g_auto) #>  #> -- Grouped Layout (grouped) ------------------------------ #>   n units   :  6  #>   n regions :  3  #>   mode      :  auto  #>   alpha_l   :  2.4 km  #>   p         :  1.25  #>   kappa     :  1.8  #>   padding   :  50 km names(g_auto) #> [1] "sf_orig"        "sf_local"       "sf_grouped"     "sf_grouped_wgs" #> [5] "stats"          "params"         "anchors"        "plots"          #> [9] "diagnostics" plot(g_auto)

title: Grouped layouts and anchor placement
text: mode = "auto_collision" adds Level 3 refinement. Overlapping region blocks iteratively repelled spring term pulls back toward radial targets:  anchor table reports resulting block radii coordinates:
code: g_collide <- explode_grouped(   x,   region_col = "region",   mode       = "auto_collision",   plot       = FALSE ) #> Level 1: Applying local explosion (alpha_l = 2410 m)... #> Level 2/3: Computing anchor positions (mode = auto_collision)... #> Anchor solver reached max iterations (60). Layout may have residual overlaps. #> Applying anchor displacement...  plot(g_collide) g_collide$anchors[, c("region", "block_radius", "anchor_x", "anchor_y")] #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   region block_radius anchor_x anchor_y #>   <chr>         <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 R1            3910.  -76471.      500 #> 2 R2            3910.  -53887.      500 #> 3 R3            3910.  102879.      500

title: Grouped layouts and anchor placement

title: Grouped layouts and anchor placement
text: local explosion stage (Level 1) preserves package’s core geometric guarantees: exact feature-level geometry preservation (Proposition 1), radial ordering within regions (Proposition 2), bounded displacement (Proposition 3). grouped anchor stage (Levels 2–3) higher-level layout extension. preserves grouping structure directional correspondence rather topological coverage.

title: Reproducing the paper examples
text: vignette reproduces key examples : Arthur, G. “Hierarchical Vector-Based Framework Multi-Scale Exploded-View Cartography.” section corresponds result paper uses package API directly, including explode_state(), explode_sf(), explode_grouped(), layout_regions(), calibration_row(). vignette designed reproduction rather routine package checks. Several sections download external boundary files may take substantial time disk space. Heavy chunks therefore marked eval = FALSE. Reported values match paper within rounding tolerance. Externally downloaded datasets may introduce small differences source files change time.

title: Reproducing the paper examples
text: paper reports αl\alpha_l stable ±15%\pm 15\% perturbation: Expected output: Mean displacement CV across ±15%\pm 15\% range <0.02< 0.02, confirming stability.
code: alpha_l_canonical <- 12447 alpha_r_canonical <- 20174  factors <- c(0.85, 0.90, 0.95, 1.00, 1.05, 1.10, 1.15) labels  <- c("-15%", "-10%", "-5%", "canonical", "+5%", "+10%", "+15%")  rows <- list() for (i in seq_along(factors)) {   run <- explode_state(     state_fips = "42", crs = 26918,     region_map = pa_region_map,     alpha_r = alpha_r_canonical,     alpha_l = round(alpha_l_canonical * factors[i]),     plot = FALSE, export = FALSE,     label = paste0("PA ", labels[i])   )   rows[[i]] <- calibration_row(run)   rows[[i]]$label <- labels[i]   rows[[i]]$factor <- factors[i] }  sensitivity_df <- bind_rows(rows) print(sensitivity_df)

title: Reproducing the paper examples
text: state registry inst/registries/state_registry.R contains New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Ohio, New York. calibration runner processes registered states reports gamma stability. Expected output (approximate): * Implied known ground-truth parameters. key finding γl=1.136\gamma_l = 1.136 stable across states, γr\gamma_r varies , indicating regional clearance still benefits dataset-specific visual validation.
code: source(system.file("registries/state_registry.R", package = "explodemap"))  calib_rows <- list() for (key in names(state_registry)) {   reg <- state_registry[[key]]    result <- tryCatch(     explode_state(       state_fips = reg$fips, crs = reg$crs,       region_map = reg$region_map,       allow_other = TRUE, plot = FALSE,       label = reg$name     ),     error = function(e) {       message("ERROR: ", e$message)       NULL     }   )    if (is.null(result)) next   calib_rows[[key]] <- calibration_row(result) }  calib_df <- bind_rows(calib_rows) print(calib_df)

title: Reproducing the paper examples
text: Canada validation tests whether framework transfers outside US administrative system entirely. Data comes Statistics Canada 2021 Census Subdivisions. Expected output: tightness ratio order magnitude larger US state examples Canadian CSDs include vast northern municipalities. formula-derived parameters still produce coherent layout, illustrating method can remain usable even extreme tightness-ratio regimes.
code: province_regions <- data.frame(   PRUID  = c("10", "11", "12", "13", "24", "35",              "46", "47", "48", "59", "60", "61", "62"),   region = c(rep("Atlantic", 4), "Quebec", "Ontario",              rep("Prairies", 3), "Pacific",              rep("Territories", 3)),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) cache_file <- file.path(path.expand("~"), "explode_map_cache",                         "canada_csds_2021.rds")  if (file.exists(cache_file)) {   sf_raw <- readRDS(cache_file) } else {   url <- paste0(     "https://www12.statcan.gc.ca/census-recensement/2021/geo/sip-pis/",     "boundary-limites/files-fichiers/lcsd000b21a_e.zip"   )   tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".zip")   download.file(url, tmp, mode = "wb")   dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "canada_csds")   dir.create(dir, showWarnings = FALSE)   unzip(tmp, exdir = dir)   shp <- list.files(dir, "\\.shp$", recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)   sf_raw <- st_read(shp[1], quiet = TRUE)   dir.create(dirname(cache_file), showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)   saveRDS(sf_raw, cache_file) } sf_proj <- sf_raw |>   st_transform(3347) |>   left_join(province_regions, by = "PRUID")  sf_proj$region[is.na(sf_proj$region)] <- "Other"  sf_prov <- sf_proj |>   filter(region != "Territories")  canada <- explode_sf(   sf_prov,   region_col = "region",   allow_other = TRUE,   label = "Canada (provinces)" )  summary(canada) plot(canada, "both")

title: Reproducing the paper examples
text: three-level extension places US states 10 HHS region blocks using anchor-based placement collision refinement. Expected output: anchor solver converges within 60 iterations. 10 HHS regions separated recognisable continental arrangement. auto_collision mode produces substantially legible output auto alone spring-repulsion solver reduces block overlaps densely packed Northeast corridor.
code: hhs_lookup <- data.frame(   STUSPS = c(     "CT", "ME", "MA", "NH", "RI", "VT",     "NJ", "NY", "PR", "VI",     "DE", "DC", "MD", "PA", "VA", "WV",     "AL", "FL", "GA", "KY", "MS", "NC", "SC", "TN",     "IL", "IN", "MI", "MN", "OH", "WI",     "AR", "LA", "NM", "OK", "TX",     "IA", "KS", "MO", "NE",     "CO", "MT", "ND", "SD", "UT", "WY",     "AZ", "CA", "HI", "NV", "GU", "AS", "MP",     "AK", "ID", "OR", "WA"   ),   hhs_region = c(     rep("1", 6), rep("2", 4), rep("3", 6), rep("4", 8),     rep("5", 6), rep("6", 5), rep("7", 4), rep("8", 6),     rep("9", 7), rep("10", 4)   ),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) cache_file <- file.path(path.expand("~"), "explode_map_cache",                         "us_states.rds")  if (file.exists(cache_file)) {   states_sf <- readRDS(cache_file) } else {   url <- "https://www2.census.gov/geo/tiger/TIGER2024/STATE/tl_2024_us_state.zip"   tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".zip")   download.file(url, tmp, mode = "wb", quiet = TRUE)   dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "us_states")   dir.create(dir, showWarnings = FALSE)   unzip(tmp, exdir = dir)   shp <- list.files(dir, "\\.shp$", recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)   states_sf <- st_read(shp[1], quiet = TRUE)   dir.create(dirname(cache_file), showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)   saveRDS(states_sf, cache_file) } states_proj <- states_sf |>   st_transform(5070) |>   left_join(hhs_lookup, by = "STUSPS")  states_proj$hhs_region[is.na(states_proj$hhs_region)] <- "Other"  hhs <- explode_grouped(   states_proj,   region_col   = "hhs_region",   mode         = "auto_collision",   alpha_l      = 120000,   p            = 1.25,   kappa        = 1.8,   padding      = 80000,   delta        = 20000,   lambda       = 0.18,   eta          = 0.18,   padding_sep  = 30000,   max_iter     = 60,   label        = "US by HHS Region" )  print(hhs) plot(hhs, "all")

title: explodemap
text: explodemap provides tools hierarchical exploded-view cartography dense administrative boundary data. package generates exploded maps applying rigid-body translations polygon geometries, separating units within across regions preserving feature’s internal geometry exactly. supports two-level core workflow described paper three-level grouped layout extension larger multi-region national displays. methodology implemented described : Arthur, G. Hierarchical Vector-Based Framework Multi-Scale Exploded-View Cartography: Centroid-Driven Spatial Displacement Dense Administrative Maps.

title: explodemap
text: explodemap supports four main workflows: explode projected sf polygon dataset using grouping column explode U.S. municipal county subdivision data directly TIGER/Line via explode_state() generate three-level grouped layouts national multi-region displays using explode_grouped() add interactive selected-area focus, labels, information cards using focus_map() htmlwidgets Shiny package also includes analytical parameter derivation, cross-dataset calibration helpers, optional bounded collision refinement dense municipal cores, optional TopoJSON export downstream tools Power BI. compact overview, see cheatsheet workflow guide.

code: library(sf) library(explodemap)  sq <- function(xmin, ymin, size = 1000) {   st_polygon(list(matrix(     c(       xmin, ymin,       xmin + size, ymin,       xmin + size, ymin + size,       xmin, ymin + size,       xmin, ymin     ),     ncol = 2,     byrow = TRUE   ))) }  geom <- st_sfc(   sq(0, 0), sq(3000, 0),      # Region A   sq(12000, 0), sq(15000, 0), # Region B   crs = 3857 )  x <- st_sf(   id = c("a1", "a2", "b1", "b2"),   region = c("A", "A", "B", "B"),   geometry = geom )  result <- explode_sf(x, region_col = "region", plot = FALSE)  print(result) plot(result, "both")

title: explodemap
text: two-level core workflow, package implements analytical parameter formulas described paper: alpha_r = gamma_r * w_bar / (2 * sin(pi / n_regions)) alpha_l = gamma_l * 2 * R_local / sqrt(n_bar) Default coefficients : gamma_r = 3.0 gamma_l = 1.136 p = 1.25 geometric quantities gamma coefficients computed dataset . gamma coefficients dimensionless legibility constants calibrated New Jersey validated across multiple U.S. states Canada example. two-level core, paper states three key properties: grouped three-level extension preserves structural grouping directional correspondence higher levels rather topological coverage.

title: explodemap
text: Two-level parameters derived automatically via Analytical Results 1–2: alpha_r = gamma_r * w_bar / (2 * sin(pi / n_regions)) — regional separation alpha_l = gamma_l * 2 * R_local / sqrt(n_bar) — local expansion Defaults: gamma_r = 3.0, gamma_l = 1.136, p = 1.25 quantities except gamma_r gamma_l computed dataset geometry. gamma coefficients dimensionless legibility constants calibrated paper examples intended practical defaults. can override alpha_r alpha_l independently particular map needs less visual separation. dense municipal cores, can add bounded collision-refinement pass analytical displacement: optional layer nudges close -region neighbors apart capping extra correction per feature. Use refine_within = "" remaining crowding crosses region boundaries.
code: refined <- explode_sf(   my_sf,   region_col = "district",   refine = TRUE,   refine_min_gap = 250,   refine_max_shift = 150 )

title: explodemap 0.2.0
text: Added three-level grouped layout engine via explode_grouped(), supporting "auto", "auto_collision", "manual" anchor modes multi-region national-scale layouts. Added layout_regions() compute region anchors standalone step custom grouped-layout workflows. Added estimate_block_radii() estimate per-region block radii using 85th percentile unit--centroid distances. Added export_topojson() optional TopoJSON export via external mapshaper CLI tool, downstream tools Power BI. Added summary.grouped_exploded_map() grouped-layout diagnostics, including local displacement parameters, anchor parameters, block radii. Added optional bounded collision refinement explode_sf() explode_state() dense municipal cores, diagnostics stored returned refinement component.

title: explodemap 0.2.0
text: Centroid handling refactored throughout package avoid sf attribute warnings centroid calculations. compute_stats() now accepts centroid_fun geometry statistics displacement use centroid semantics. explode_sf() explode_state() now respect partial manual overrides: users may set alpha_r alpha_l deriving parameter geometry. Direction vectors distance scaling now handle true zero-distance cases without suppressing sub-metre coordinate differences. Grouped anchor collision refinement now deterministic coincident anchors, improving reproducibility. Manual grouped anchors now validate every region one anchor regions duplicated anchor table. Grouped objects now inherit grouped_exploded_map exploded_map, improving method consistency. Grouped S3 methods consolidated cleaned consistent printing, plotting, summary output. Distance formatting diagnostic output now adapts metres kilometres, improving readability small synthetic examples real-world datasets. Focus maps now support non-blocking info cards, selected-area sizing controls, denser-layer performance tuning Shiny workflows. validate_input() now returns visibly, making repaired geometries less fragile internal workflows.

title: explodemap 0.2.0
text: Added three vignettes: getting started, grouped layouts, paper replication. Added small installed examples basic sf workflow, optional collision refinement, lookup-table grouping, manual parameter tuning. Added workflow-selection vignette site-linked cheatsheet release-oriented user guidance. Added inst/CITATION entry academic citation. Added pkgdown site configuration GitHub Pages support.

title: explodemap 0.1.0
text: Added core two-level displacement engine exploded-view cartography dense administrative boundary data. Added three public entry points: explode_sf(), explode_sf_with_lookup(), explode_state(). Added analytical parameter derivation via derive_params(), implementing Analytical Results 1 2. Added geometry statistics via compute_stats(). Added exploded_map S3 class print(), plot(), summary() methods. Added calibration_row() cross-dataset calibration tables. Added TIGER/Line download caching support via explode_state(). Added state registry New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Ohio, New York configurations. Added example workflows cross-state calibration, Canada validation, HHS grouped layouts.
